payload.claim_text: Our newest buses (delivered since 2021) are 2 to 3 times more reliable than our older fleets.
payload.description: Reliability comparison between oldest and newest bus fleets.

records.display_name: IEC Concurrence on Bus Procurement Strategy

payload.claim_text: The IEC concurs with the MTA strategy of not accepting buses until all technical and quality issues are resolved and meet contract requirements.
payload.description: IEC position on bus acceptance strategy.

records.display_name: Battery Life Concern for New Flyer BEBs

payload.claim_text: Battery life for the 60 New Flyer BEBs has been a concern. The technical specifications mandate an expected 8-year battery life.
payload.description: IEC observation that battery life for New Flyer BEBs has been a concern.

payload.claim_text: 1,753 buses are currently 12 years old, or older, which will be replaced in these Capital Programs.
payload.description: Count of buses at or exceeding 12 years old that are slated for replacement.

payload.claim_text: Nearly half of the $68 billion plan has already been initiated through C&D's processes.
payload.description: Budget agreement in Albany authorizes full funding for the 2025-2029 Capital Plan.

records.display_name: Rockaway Service Restoration On Time and On Budget

payload.claim_text: The successful restoration of service to the Rockaways following a full 17-week shutdown was delivered on time and on budget.
payload.description: MTA C&D President highlighted the Rockaway service restoration project achievement.

payload.claim_text: Work on Park Avenue Viaduct span replacements, thanks to Board-approved changes in contract approach, is expected to save nearly four years of construction time and over $90 million.
payload.description: Savings from revised contract approach on Park Avenue Viaduct span replacements.

records.display_name: IEC Concern on BEB Acceptance Backlog

payload.claim_text: The IEC is concerned this may affect the acceptance into service of the upcoming delivery of 205 BEBs currently projected to begin in October 2025.
payload.description: IEC concern that delays in resolving technical and quality issues could affect BEB acceptance.

records.display_name: B&T Verrazzano Narrows Bridge Main Cable Dehumidification

description: Main cable dehumidification system for Verrazzano Narrows Bridge

Design-Build services for installation of cable dehumidification system on the four main cables of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, including acoustic monitoring, electrical and communication systems, and five-year maintenance.

Installation of a cable dehumidification system on the four main cables of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge to prevent corrosion and preserve cable strength, including acoustic monitoring, electrical systems, cable inspection, and maintenance for five years after commissioning.

description: Purchase of R211 B-Division subway cars including base order of 535 cars ($1.75B) and Option 1 exercised for 640 additional cars ($3.67B total budget)
R211 program includes 535-car base order (440 R211A, 75 R211S, 20 R211T) plus Option 1 of 640 R211A cars exercised November 2022. Supplier: Kawasaki.

Base order of 535 cars (440 R211A, 75 R211S, 20 R211T) plus Option 1 of 640 R211A cars exercised. Budget $3.67B. Supplier: Kawasaki. R211A base order completion forecast Apr 2025. R211S target start of 30-day in-service test mid-Aug 2024.

Hundreds of brand-new R211 cars entered service in 2024, now making up the majority of the A line fleet. Nation's first open-gangway cars in service on the C line. 320 R211 cars in service, with more on the way.
Purchase of R211A, R211S (Staten Island Railway), and R211T open gangway subway cars from Kawasaki. Base order of 535 cars plus Option 1 (640 R211A) and Option 2 (355 R211A + 80 R211T).

R211 subway car program with up to 1,612 cars for NYCT and SIR. Base order of 535 cars: 440 R211A, 75 R211S, 20 R211T open gangway test cars. Base order budget $1.75 B. Awarded to Kawasaki in February 2018.

R211 contract awarded to Kawasaki in February 2018 for up to 1,612 cars for NYCT and Staten Island Railway. Base order: 535 cars, budgeted at $1.75B, comprising 440 closed-end cars, 75 SIR cars, and 20 open-gangway test cars. Replacing R46 (40+ years old) and SIR R44 (close to 50 years old) cars. Forecast delivery of all vehicles moved from July 2023 to January 2025 (18-month delay).
status: In progress - Base Order Completion January 2025

description: New bus procurement program covering clean-diesel, hybrid, battery-electric, articulated, and express buses across NYCT/DOB and MTA Bus Company.
2020-2024 Capital Program Contracts for nearly 700 buses across 6 fleet types with total budget over $570M
MTA NYCT Department of Buses procures buses for NYCT and MTA Bus service. 2020-2024 Capital Program totals 2,161 buses. Includes clean-diesel, CNG, BEB, and articulated buses.

Bus fleet procurement program. Current fleet of approximately 5,800 buses at 28 depots city-wide. 1,776 buses from 2015-2019 Capital Program awarded (89% accepted). 706 buses from 2020-2024 Capital Program under contract. Plans to solicit 470 Battery-Electric Buses (including 90 articulated) by end of 2022. All contracts on budget.
